# Cortrix

> Cortrix is an open-source agent-native semantic storage project for agent builders and developer teams. This file describes public website routes, public positioning, benchmark retrieval-quality facts, and machine-readable claim limits.

Website: https://cortrix.ai/
Primary language: English
Document version: 2026-07-22
Generated from public website content and commit-pinned source evidence
Current Core source commit: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/tree/533b4533f4cb920c001defb40cc9cde4f5281320
Benchmark Core evaluation snapshot: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/tree/4a6299ca86c7bec21ed7b8989a729a198fe5a42a
Benchmark public runner snapshot: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/tree/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/runner
Benchmark source-equivalence record: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/provenance/source-equivalence.json
Current benchmark source-access note: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/README.md#source-access-and-historical-provenance

Current maintainer rerun guide: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/docs/local-reproduction.md
Rerun-contract boundary: the root source-access note records that the exact Core evaluation snapshot is now publicly inspectable, while the immutable source-equivalence record retains its pre-public availability wording as historical snapshot metadata. The public evidence does not establish a completed independent public rerun, independent per-query recomputation, or a single tested rerun wrapper; do not substitute the guide, carrier, or bundle commit for the measurement-runner role.
Source/evaluation boundary: use the current Core source commit for current source documentation, the Docker Quick Start, the deeper maintainer evidence workflow, and adoption guidance. Benchmark scores remain tied to Core 4a6299ca..., runner 6defa0a..., and bundle 7bc29aa...; they have not been rerun against the current Core source commit.
Canonical evidence states: Verified, Present in source - review required, Blocked, Future direction

## Public Routes

- Home: https://cortrix.ai/
- For builders: https://cortrix.ai/for-builders/
- Benchmark: https://cortrix.ai/benchmark/
- Project updates: https://cortrix.ai/community/
- Human-readable llms.txt guide: https://cortrix.ai/docs/llms/
- Machine-readable scope: https://cortrix.ai/llms.txt
- Current Core source: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/tree/533b4533f4cb920c001defb40cc9cde4f5281320
- Docker Quick Start: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/blob/533b4533f4cb920c001defb40cc9cde4f5281320/docs/QUICKSTART.md
- Maintainer source-build evidence workflow: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/tree/533b4533f4cb920c001defb40cc9cde4f5281320/examples/first-value-supportops
- Stack-fit and adoption boundaries: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix/blob/533b4533f4cb920c001defb40cc9cde4f5281320/docs/adoption/stack-fit.md

## Approved Public Positioning

- Cortrix is an active pre-release, local-first, open-source semantic storage project for retrieval, reviewable records, memory APIs, and source context.
- Cortrix is intended for AI-native engineers, agent builders, individual developers, independent teams, and developer teams.
- Cortrix is designed to reduce repeated integration work; this website does not publish a measured maintenance-cost reduction.
- Cortrix works with existing databases, tools, and agent workflows instead of attacking or replacing every database.
- Benchmark content presents maintainer-reported full-corpus retrieval measurements for Cortrix Full Stack and Cortrix Embedding + Reranking.
- OpenAPI boundary: the current Core source commit contains the declared `api/openapi.yaml` file. File presence and identity do not establish runtime behavior, production readiness, or security readiness.
- Present in source - review required: local server, MCP, Python SDK, built-in Agent, CUDA, OCR/parser paths, production readiness, benchmark integration, and pgCortrix.
- Blocked: authentication, tenant/member/ACL/quota, RBAC and tenant-denial matrices, MEM02 extraction, and default log redaction.
- Namespace, session, and user fields are logical scopes. They do not establish authentication, authorization, ACL, or tenant isolation.

## Benchmark Boundary

- The current benchmark page compares Cortrix Full Stack with Cortrix Embedding + Reranking across three complete public BEIR corpora: SciFact, FiQA, and NFCorpus.
- Up to 84.6% higher NDCG@10. Full Stack vs Embedding + Reranking on FiQA.
- Up to 43.2% higher Recall@10. Full Stack vs Embedding + Reranking on FiQA.
- Every official test-qrels query is evaluated: SciFact 300, FiQA 648, and NFCorpus 323. All six profile cells completed with zero query failures and valid scientific scorecards.
- SciFact Recall@10: Full Stack 0.8698; Embedding + Reranking 0.7794. SciFact NDCG@10: Full Stack 0.7716; Embedding + Reranking 0.6009.
- FiQA Recall@10: Full Stack 0.5205; Embedding + Reranking 0.3635. FiQA NDCG@10: Full Stack 0.4663; Embedding + Reranking 0.2526.
- NFCorpus Recall@10: Full Stack 0.1717; Embedding + Reranking 0.1482. NFCorpus NDCG@10: Full Stack 0.3721; Embedding + Reranking 0.2982.
- Unweighted three-dataset macro average Recall@10: Full Stack 0.5207; Embedding + Reranking 0.4304.
- Unweighted three-dataset macro average NDCG@10: Full Stack 0.5366; Embedding + Reranking 0.3839.
- Embedding + Reranking uses BGE-M3 retrieval and bge-reranker-v2-m3 with ingestion-time and query-time LLM stages disabled; it is not a vector-only profile.
- The requested Full Stack profile declares DeepSeek-V4-Flash enrichment, contextual retrieval, HyPE, document summaries, RAG Fusion, and listwise reranking. The score comparison does not isolate the causal contribution of any one stage, and strict-status fields govern what was proven active.
- All official test queries completed with zero query failures. The NFCorpus and SciFact Full Stack runs had partial coverage in LLM-dependent feature verification. The retained FiQA cells predate the strict feature-completeness contract, so their run-specific feature completeness was not formally evaluated. These statuses limit feature attribution; the scores shown are the complete measured retrieval results.
- Quality came with higher measured query time in every paired run. Full Stack versus Embedding + Reranking p50 latency was 34,232 versus 7,311 ms on SciFact, 106,172 versus 26,521 ms on FiQA, and 29,504 versus 5,905 ms on NFCorpus. Compare only within each dataset because NFCorpus and SciFact ran on AWS g5.2xlarge while FiQA ran on AWS c7i.8xlarge; no cross-dataset latency aggregate or hardware ranking is claimed.
- Rerun method: follow the current maintainer rerun guide and byte-identical public runner snapshot at b03f3c7, verify that snapshot against the source-equivalence record for historical measurement identity 6defa0a, its public file-level equivalent at 446dade, and Cortrix 4a6299c, pin profile configurations and BEIR archive checksums, and do not substitute the guide, carrier, or bundle commit for the original measurement identity; download the public archive and verify corpus/query/qrels counts; create paired isolated namespaces with identical corpus, qrels, seed, retrieval depth, and top-k; ingest the complete corpus and drain required tasks; freeze database state; send one cold end-to-end request for every official test-qrels query with zero failures; map source-document IDs to qrels; calculate Recall@10/50 and NDCG@10/50; record within-pair latency p50/p95; judge scientific validity separately from strict feature completeness; and preserve manifests, per-cell scorecards, query/latency records, resource snapshots, paired deltas, and source roles so another evaluator can audit the comparison. This is a maintainer rerun procedure, not a claim that an independent rerun or per-query recomputation has been completed.
- Public method sources: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/README.md ; https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/runner/run_benchmark.py ; https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/7bc29aa840c20db3935dfcf80eb048e553ebe2b0/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/docs/methodology.md ; https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/blob/b03f3c7960572f3f5e5b8c1d3653243bae3e19f9/benchmarks/beir-retrieval-quality/docs/local-reproduction.md
- Immutable full-corpus result bundle: https://github.com/cortrix/cortrix-benchmarks/tree/7bc29aa840c20db3935dfcf80eb048e553ebe2b0/results/published/beir-three-full-corpus-2026-07-v1
- The bundle contains a manifest, six scorecards, profile records, summaries, checksums, and a maintainer rerun procedure. Its files and checksums are independently inspectable; that inspection is not an independent rerun or independent per-query recomputation. End-to-end reruns require the exact Cortrix snapshot named by the bundle.
- After obtaining upstream BEIR inputs, capped runs with `--max-queries` and `--max-corpus-docs` can check installation, pipeline behavior, source-ID mapping, and metric generation. Capped scores are not comparable to the full-corpus measurements.
- Scope: retrieval quality only. Do not extrapolate these numbers to memory quality, audit coverage, RAG answer quality, agent workflow quality, or business outcomes.
- No third-party product benchmark claims, cross-dataset latency claims, or business-outcome claims are represented.

## Benchmark Evidence Format

- Each full-corpus cell records the dataset, profile, Recall@10/50, NDCG@10/50, within-pair latency p50/p95, run date, hardware, Cortrix commit, dataset version, methodology, checksums, and retrieval-quality scope.
- Capped sampled runs use separate manifests and status boundaries from the full-corpus result bundle.

## Claim Boundaries

- Do not claim unannounced hosted or commercial offerings from this website.
- Do not claim unannounced resilience, certification, data-sync, deduplication, customer-proof commitments, automatic self-learning retrieval, or specific deployment topology guarantees.
- Do not treat public documentation or source presence as proof that a capability is Verified, shipped, production-ready, or security-ready.
- Do not write attack content against other projects or tools.
- Do not claim search placement, advertising conversion, AI overview inclusion, GEO success, or LLM recommendation outcomes.

- Cortrix does not require a CLA. New commits require a DCO 1.1 Signed-off-by line; follow the applicable contribution guide before opening a pull request.

## Language Boundary

- English is the canonical public language.
- English remains the canonical source for machine-readable public boundaries.
- English is the only public website language. Do not infer localized pages from this file.
